new_value.name: Section 301 Tariff Pipeline
new_value.description: USTR launched 76 new Section 301 investigations targeting forced labor and excess capacity across dozens of economies. Section 301 is a statutory trade instrument substantially harder to challenge in court than IEEPA, representing a regime-level reconfiguration of US tariff legal architecture. The administration is using Section 122 balance-of-payments authority as a 150-day bridge, but the Section 301 pipeline represents a durable multi-year tariff enforcement framework. The 150-day Section 122 bridge expires mid-2026, creating a critical path event.

new_value.name: Fiscal Dominance Tail Risk
new_value.description: US fiscal maturity wall $9.8T plus $1T interest burden meets tariff-fueled inflation; forces fiscal dominance tail risk where the Fed is forced to accommodate fiscal needs by easing despite inflation. CBO FY2026 numbers plus tariff pass-through break Fed independence tail risk. Bank reserves $3.2T cushion thinning. This is under-covered by consensus, which remains focused on Iran war dynamics.

new_value.name: Hormuz Energy Shock
new_value.description: Hormuz transit 5-10 ships per day versus 138 normal despite fragile ceasefire. Brent crude $104 approaching Oxford Economics contraction threshold. This is a primary CRISIS driver corroborated by reasoner. Reasoner confirms Hormuz fragility as SA structural signal, not TS episodic.

patches_actually_applied.rationale: TA-001 state changed from WATCH to WARNING due to Section 301 launch and BOP bridge expiry critical path
